name: Migration Parser & Planner
description: Parses SQL migration files (PostgreSQL/ANSI SQL) using regex-based extraction to identify ADD COLUMN, DROP COLUMN, and ALTER COLUMN operations from ALTER TABLE statements. Computes schema diffs against the current registry state and applies gate logic to produce migration plans. Gate decisions are ordered AUTO_PROCEED < HUMAN_GATE < BLOCKED. Plans are persisted as JSON in .ledger/plans/ with UUID-based plan IDs. Supports human approval workflow for HUMAN_GATE plans with required review reference and rationale. Blast radius computed via single-hop FK expansion from registry annotations.

types.name: OperationType
types.description: Discriminator for the type of column operation extracted from SQL.

types.name: GateDecision
types.description: Ordered severity enum for migration gate outcomes. AUTO_PROCEED < HUMAN_GATE < BLOCKED.

types.name: PlanStatus
types.description: Lifecycle status of a migration plan.

types.name: ViolationSeverity
types.description: Severity level for gate violations, aligned with GateDecision.

types.name: ColumnConstraint

fields.name: constraint_type
fields.description: The type of constraint, e.g. 'NOT_NULL', 'DEFAULT', 'UNIQUE', 'PRIMARY_KEY', 'REFERENCES', 'CHECK'.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Optional constraint value, e.g. the default expression or referenced table.column for REFERENCES.

types.description: A single constraint parsed from a column definition in SQL (e.g., NOT NULL, DEFAULT, UNIQUE, PRIMARY KEY, REFERENCES).

types.name: ColumnOperation

fields.name: op_type
fields.description: Discriminator: ADD_COLUMN, DROP_COLUMN, or ALTER_COLUMN.

fields.name: table_name
fields.description: Fully qualified or unqualified table name from the ALTER TABLE statement.

fields.name: column_name
fields.description: Name of the column being added, dropped, or altered.

fields.name: new_type
fields.description: The SQL type for ADD_COLUMN or the new type for ALTER_COLUMN. Empty for DROP_COLUMN.

fields.name: old_type
fields.description: The previous SQL type for ALTER_COLUMN (populated from registry context during diff). Empty for ADD/DROP.

fields.name: constraints
fields.description: Parsed constraints for the column (NOT NULL, DEFAULT, etc.). Empty list if none.

types.description: A single column-level operation extracted from an ALTER TABLE SQL statement. Discriminated by op_type field.

types.name: ParseWarning

fields.name: line_number
fields.description: 1-based line number in the source SQL where the warning occurred.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the parse warning.

fields.name: raw_statement
fields.description: The raw SQL statement fragment that triggered the warning.

types.description: A non-fatal warning emitted during SQL parsing, e.g., unrecognized statement or ambiguous syntax.

types.name: ParsedMigration

fields.name: operations
fields.description: Ordered list of column operations extracted from the SQL file.

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: Filesystem path of the source SQL migration file.

fields.name: source_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the raw SQL content for integrity tracking.

fields.name: statement_count
fields.description: Total number of semicolon-delimited statements found in the file (including non-ALTER statements).

fields.name: warnings
fields.description: Non-fatal parse warnings. Empty if parsing was clean.

types.description: Result of parsing a SQL migration file. Contains all extracted column operations, source metadata, and any parse warnings.

types.name: FieldAnnotation

fields.name: classification_tier
fields.description: Data classification tier: PUBLIC, INTERNAL, PII, RESTRICTED.

fields.name: is_audit_field
fields.description: Whether this field is marked as an audit field in the registry.

fields.name: is_immutable
fields.description: Whether this field is marked as immutable in the registry.

fields.name: is_encrypted
fields.description: Whether this field is marked as requiring encryption in the registry.

types.description: Registry-sourced annotation context for a specific field, used during diff enrichment and gate evaluation.

types.name: DiffEntry

fields.name: operation
fields.description: The parsed column operation from SQL.

fields.name: annotation
fields.description: Registry-sourced annotation for this field. None if the field is new (ADD) or not found in registry.

fields.name: is_new_field
fields.description: True if this is an ADD_COLUMN for a field not currently in the registry.

fields.name: is_field_removal
fields.description: True if this is a DROP_COLUMN for a field that exists in the registry.

types.description: A single operation enriched with registry context. Combines the parsed SQL operation with annotation metadata from the schema registry.

types.name: SchemaDiff

fields.name: entries
fields.description: Ordered list of enriched diff entries.

fields.name: affected_tables
fields.description: Deduplicated list of table names directly affected by the migration operations.

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: Source migration file path, propagated from ParsedMigration.

fields.name: source_hash
fields.description: Source file hash, propagated from ParsedMigration.

types.description: Complete diff between a parsed migration and the current registry state, enriched with annotation context.

types.name: GateViolation

fields.name: rule_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the gate rule that was violated (e.g., 'audit_field_drop', 'immutable_modify', 'encryption_removal').

fields.name: severity
fields.description: The gate decision severity for this violation: BLOCKED, HUMAN_GATE, or AUTO_PROCEED.

fields.name: table_name
fields.description: The table containing the violating field.

fields.name: column_name
fields.description: The column that triggered the violation.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable explanation of why this violation was raised.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Additional structured context (e.g., current tier, requested tier, annotation details).

types.description: A single gate rule violation detected during migration gate evaluation.

types.name: GateRuleEntry

fields.name: condition_type
fields.description: The condition identifier, e.g., 'audit_field_drop', 'immutable_modify', 'encryption_removal', 'tier_mismatch', 'public_only_change'.

fields.name: decision
fields.description: The gate decision to apply when this condition is matched.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of the rule for audit and display purposes.

types.description: A single row in the gate rule data table mapping a condition type and field annotation pattern to a gate decision.

types.name: ComponentContext

fields.name: component_id
fields.description: The component_id of the component whose migration is being evaluated.

fields.name: declared_data_access_tiers
fields.description: The list of data classification tiers this component has declared in its data_access (e.g., ['PUBLIC', 'INTERNAL']).

types.description: Context about the component requesting the migration, used to evaluate tier-based gate rules.

types.name: MigrationPlan

fields.name: plan_id
fields.description: UUID v4 string uniquely identifying this plan.

validators.error_message: plan_id must be a valid UUID v4

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: Version counter for the plan schema format, for forward-compatible deserialization.

fields.name: diff
fields.description: The enriched schema diff this plan was computed from.

fields.name: violations
fields.description: All gate violations detected. May be empty for AUTO_PROCEED plans.

fields.name: overall_gate
fields.description: The highest-severity gate decision across all violations. AUTO_PROCEED if no violations.

fields.name: blast_radius
fields.description: Set of table names in the blast radius: directly affected tables plus single-hop FK-referenced tables.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current lifecycle status of the plan.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of plan creation.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last status update.

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: Original migration SQL file path.

fields.name: source_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of the original SQL file for integrity verification.

types.description: A complete migration plan: diff, violations, overall gate decision, blast radius, and lifecycle status. Persisted as JSON in .ledger/plans/<plan_id>.json.

types.name: ApprovalRecord

fields.name: plan_id
fields.description: UUID of the plan being approved.

validators.error_message: plan_id must be a valid UUID v4

fields.name: reviewer
fields.description: Identity of the reviewer approving the plan.

fields.name: review_reference
fields.description: External review reference (e.g., ticket ID, PR URL) passed via --review flag.

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: Human-written rationale for why this migration is approved despite gate violations.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of approval.

fields.name: new_status
fields.description: The status the plan transitions to (APPROVED).

types.description: Records human approval of a HUMAN_GATE migration plan, including the required review reference and rationale.

types.name: MigrationParseError

fields.name: source_path
fields.description: Path to the SQL file that failed to parse.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error description.

fields.name: line_number
fields.description: Line number where the fatal error occurred, if applicable.

fields.name: raw_content
fields.description: The raw content fragment that caused the failure.

types.description: Raised when SQL parsing fails catastrophically (e.g., file not found, encoding error, completely unparseable content). Contains structured context.

types.name: PlanNotFoundError

fields.name: plan_id
fields.description: The UUID that was not found.

fields.name: search_path
fields.description: The filesystem path that was searched.

types.description: Raised when a plan_id does not correspond to any persisted plan in .ledger/plans/.

types.name: InvalidPlanTransitionError

fields.name: plan_id
fields.description: The UUID of the plan with the invalid transition.

fields.name: current_status
fields.description: The current status of the plan.

fields.name: requested_status
fields.description: The status that was requested.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Explanation of why the transition is invalid.

types.description: Raised when an attempted plan status transition is not valid (e.g., approving an already-approved plan or approving a BLOCKED plan).

types.name: PlanPersistenceError

fields.name: plan_id
fields.description: The UUID of the plan that failed to persist.

fields.name: target_path
fields.description: The filesystem path where the write was attempted.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error description.

types.description: Raised when atomic file write for plan persistence fails (disk full, permission denied, etc.).

functions.name: parse_migration
functions.description: Parses a SQL migration file using regex-based extraction targeting PostgreSQL/ANSI SQL. Strips comments (single-line -- and block /* */), splits on semicolons, and extracts ADD COLUMN, DROP COLUMN, and ALTER COLUMN operations from ALTER TABLE statements. Returns all operations and warnings; never short-circuits on the first warning.

inputs.name: sql
inputs.description: Raw SQL content of the migration file.

inputs.name: source_path
inputs.description: Filesystem path of the source SQL file, for error reporting and metadata.

error_cases.name: empty_sql
error_cases.condition: sql is empty or contains only whitespace/comments

error_data.message: SQL content is empty or contains only comments

error_cases.name: encoding_error
error_cases.condition: sql contains invalid characters that prevent regex extraction

preconditions: source_path is a non-empty string representing a valid filesystem path
postconditions: Returned ParsedMigration.source_path equals the input source_path
Returned ParsedMigration.source_hash is the SHA-256 hex digest of the input sql string
Returned ParsedMigration.operations contains only ADD_COLUMN, DROP_COLUMN, or ALTER_COLUMN operations
All parse warnings are collected in ParsedMigration.warnings — parsing does not stop at first warning
Statement count reflects total semicolon-delimited statements including non-ALTER statements

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function: parses in-memory SQL string, no I/O.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the length of the SQL string

functions.name: compute_diff
functions.description: Computes a schema diff between a parsed migration and the current registry state. Each parsed operation is enriched with field annotations from the registry (classification tier, audit/immutable/encrypted flags). Produces the list of affected tables from the migration operations.

inputs.name: parsed
inputs.description: The parsed migration result from parse_migration.

inputs.name: registry
inputs.description: SchemaRegistry instance providing get_field_annotations and get_schema lookups.

error_data.message: Cannot compute diff from a migration with zero operations

error_cases.name: registry_lookup_failure
error_cases.condition: Registry raises an error when looking up schema or annotations for a table

error_data.message: Failed to retrieve registry data for table

preconditions: parsed is a valid ParsedMigration with at least one operation
registry is a valid SchemaRegistry instance with accessible schema data
postconditions: Returned SchemaDiff.entries has same length as parsed.operations
Each DiffEntry.operation corresponds to the same-index operation in parsed.operations
DiffEntry.annotation is populated from registry for existing fields, None for new fields
DiffEntry.is_new_field is True iff op_type is ADD_COLUMN and field not in registry
DiffEntry.is_field_removal is True iff op_type is DROP_COLUMN and field exists in registry
SchemaDiff.affected_tables contains all unique table names from the operations
SchemaDiff.source_path and source_hash propagated from parsed

structured_side_effects.description: Reads registry state in-memory; does not mutate registry. Registry access is via method calls, not I/O.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n * m) where n is number of operations and m is average registry lookup cost

functions.name: evaluate_gates
functions.description: Applies the gate rule data table against every entry in a SchemaDiff, using the component's declared data access tiers for tier-based rules. Returns ALL violations found — never short-circuits. Gate rules: audit_field drop → BLOCKED, immutable field modify → BLOCKED, encryption removal → HUMAN_GATE, classification tier not in component's data_access → HUMAN_GATE, PUBLIC-only change to declared-PUBLIC component → AUTO_PROCEED.

inputs.name: diff
inputs.description: The enriched schema diff to evaluate.

inputs.name: component_context
inputs.description: Context about the component requesting the migration, including declared data access tiers.

error_data.message: Cannot evaluate gates on an empty diff

preconditions: diff is a valid SchemaDiff with at least one entry
postconditions: Returned list contains all GateViolation instances — every rule is evaluated against every diff entry
Each GateViolation has a valid rule_id corresponding to a rule in the gate rule table
If no violations are found, an empty list is returned (implies AUTO_PROCEED overall)
Violations are ordered by severity descending (BLOCKED first, then HUMAN_GATE, then AUTO_PROCEED)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function: evaluates rules against in-memory diff data, no I/O.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n * r) where n is number of diff entries and r is number of gate rules

functions.name: create_plan
functions.description: Assembles a MigrationPlan from a SchemaDiff and list of GateViolations, computes the blast radius (affected tables + single-hop FK expansion from registry), assigns a UUID v4 plan_id, sets status to PENDING, and persists the plan as JSON to .ledger/plans/<plan_id>.json using atomic file write (write to temp, then rename).

inputs.name: diff
inputs.description: The enriched schema diff for this plan.

inputs.name: violations
inputs.description: All GateViolation instances from evaluate_gates. May be empty.

inputs.name: registry
inputs.description: SchemaRegistry instance for FK lookups to compute blast radius.

error_cases.name: plans_dir_not_writable
error_cases.condition: The plans_dir does not exist or is not writable

error_cases.name: atomic_write_failure
error_cases.condition: Atomic file write (temp + rename) fails due to disk error or permissions

error_data.message: Atomic write of plan file failed

error_cases.name: registry_fk_lookup_failure
error_cases.condition: Registry raises an error when looking up foreign key annotations for blast radius

error_data.message: Failed to retrieve FK annotations for blast radius computation

preconditions: violations is a list of GateViolation (may be empty)
plans_dir is a writable directory path
registry is a valid SchemaRegistry for FK lookups
postconditions: Returned MigrationPlan.plan_id is a valid UUID v4 string
Returned MigrationPlan.status is PENDING
Returned MigrationPlan.overall_gate is the max severity across all violations, or AUTO_PROCEED if violations is empty
Returned MigrationPlan.blast_radius includes all affected_tables from diff plus single-hop FK-referenced tables
Blast radius does NOT include transitive FK references (single hop only)
A JSON file exists at plans_dir/<plan_id>.json containing the serialized plan
File write is atomic: partial writes do not leave corrupt plan files
created_at and updated_at are set to the same ISO 8601 timestamp at creation time

structured_side_effects.description: Persists the migration plan as a JSON file using atomic write (write to temp file, then os.rename).
Checks plans directory exists and is writable before persisting.

functions.name: approve_plan
functions.description: Records human approval for a HUMAN_GATE migration plan. Validates the plan exists and is in PENDING status with overall_gate == HUMAN_GATE, then transitions status to APPROVED, updates the plan JSON, and appends an ApprovalRecord to the changelog. Requires a --review reference and rationale.

inputs.name: plan_id
inputs.description: UUID v4 of the plan to approve.

validators.error_message: plan_id must be a valid UUID v4

inputs.name: review_ref
inputs.description: External review reference (ticket ID, PR URL, etc.).

inputs.name: rationale
inputs.description: Human-written rationale for approving despite violations.

error_cases.name: plan_not_found
error_cases.condition: No plan file exists for the given plan_id in plans_dir

error_cases.name: plan_is_blocked
error_cases.condition: Plan overall_gate is BLOCKED — BLOCKED plans cannot be approved

error_data.message: Cannot approve a BLOCKED plan — migration must be modified to remove blocking violations

error_cases.name: plan_is_auto_proceed
error_cases.condition: Plan overall_gate is AUTO_PROCEED — does not require approval

error_data.message: AUTO_PROCEED plans do not require manual approval

error_cases.name: atomic_write_failure
error_cases.condition: Atomic file write for updated plan or changelog fails

preconditions: plan_id is a valid UUID v4
reviewer, review_ref, and rationale are non-empty strings
A plan file exists at plans_dir/<plan_id>.json
The plan's current status is PENDING
The plan's overall_gate is HUMAN_GATE (BLOCKED plans cannot be approved; AUTO_PROCEED plans do not need approval)
postconditions: Returned MigrationPlan.status is APPROVED
Returned MigrationPlan.updated_at reflects the approval timestamp
The plan JSON file at plans_dir/<plan_id>.json is updated atomically with new status
An ApprovalRecord is appended to the changelog
The plan_id, diff, violations, and overall_gate are unchanged from the original plan

structured_side_effects.description: Atomically updates the plan JSON file with APPROVED status and updated_at timestamp.
Appends a JSON-lines entry with the ApprovalRecord for audit trail.
Reads the existing plan to validate current status and overall_gate before transition.

functions.name: load_plan
functions.description: Loads a persisted MigrationPlan from .ledger/plans/<plan_id>.json. Deserializes from JSON and validates the plan schema version.

inputs.name: plan_id
inputs.description: UUID v4 of the plan to load.

validators.error_message: plan_id must be a valid UUID v4

error_cases.name: plan_not_found
error_cases.condition: No JSON file exists at plans_dir/<plan_id>.json

error_cases.name: corrupted_plan_file
error_cases.condition: The JSON file exists but cannot be deserialized into a valid MigrationPlan (invalid JSON, missing fields, schema mismatch)

error_data.message: Plan file is corrupted or has incompatible schema version

error_cases.name: file_read_error
error_cases.condition: File exists but cannot be read (permissions, encoding error)

preconditions: plan_id is a valid UUID v4
postconditions: Returned MigrationPlan.plan_id equals the input plan_id
Returned MigrationPlan passes Pydantic validation for all fields
Returned MigrationPlan.schema_version is compatible with the current code version

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and deserializes the plan JSON file from disk.

invariants: Gate decisions are totally ordered: AUTO_PROCEED < HUMAN_GATE < BLOCKED. The overall gate for a plan is always the highest-severity gate across all violations.
Plan status transitions are strictly: PENDING -> APPROVED or PENDING -> REJECTED. No other transitions are valid. APPROVED and REJECTED are terminal states.
Blast radius includes all directly affected tables from the parsed migration plus single-hop FK-referenced tables from registry annotations. Transitive FK chasing is never performed.
SQL parsing uses regex extraction only — no full SQL parser. Only ALTER TABLE with ADD COLUMN, DROP COLUMN, and ALTER COLUMN operations are recognized.
Schema YAML and SQL source content are never normalized, reformatted, or reordered during parsing.
All parse and validation functions return complete lists of warnings/violations, never short-circuiting on the first error.
Plan files are written atomically to .ledger/plans/ to prevent partial writes on crash.
Plan IDs are UUID v4, guaranteed unique per plan.
Gate rules are defined as a data table (dict mapping condition to decision), not as code branches.

data_access.rationale: Reads SQL migration files containing schema DDL (PUBLIC/INTERNAL) and reads registry field annotations which may reference PII classification tiers to evaluate gate rules. Writes migration plan JSON files and approval changelog entries to .ledger/plans/ (INTERNAL tier — plans contain schema structure metadata, gate decisions, and reviewer identities but no actual row data).

side_effects.rationale: Reads SQL migration files from disk to extract column operations via regex parsing.
Reads registry schema metadata and field annotations to enrich diffs with context needed for gate evaluation, including PII-classified field annotations.
Persists migration plan JSON and approval records to .ledger/plans/ directory with atomic file writes.
Appends approval record entries to the migration changelog when a HUMAN_GATE plan is approved.

authority.rationale: The migration component is the sole authority for creating, persisting, and transitioning the lifecycle state of migration plans and approval records stored in .ledger/plans/. No other component may write or modify plan JSON files or approval changelog entries.
